articles.title: Will Mumbai's BEST buses run today? Strike continues after talks fail, lakhs of commuters hit
articles.description: The strike severely affected public transport across Mumbai. Out of BEST's fleet of 2,766 buses, only 48 remained operational on Friday. Several buses that initially left depots were reportedly forced to return after incidents of stone-pelting and obstruction by striking workers.

articles.title: Air India crash interim report deliberately left out cockpit data? Pilot body makes major allegation
articles.description: Pilots' body Federation of Indian Pilots (FIP) has urged the government to order simulator-based reconstruction for the Air India Flight 171 crash investigation. FIP has questioned the preliminary findings, suggesting an electrical malfunction preceded engine failure, citing a lack of expected cockpit alerts.

articles.title: 'WE NEED PLOT TWISTS': Trump Nearly Fired Hegseth In Wild White House Power Drama? Shock Reveal
articles.description: A new report from The New York Times reveals just how close Pete Hegseth may have come to losing his shot at leading the Pentagon. According to the report, President Donald Trump seriously considered replacing Hegseth as Defense Secretary nominee amid mounting scrutiny and political backlash. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis reportedly emerged as a potential alternative as doubts grew over Hegseth's confirmation prospects. In a striking detail, Trump allegedly told aides, "We need plot twists," while discussions unfolded behind closed doors. Despite the uncertainty, Trump ultimately stood by Hegseth, working behind the scenes to save the nomination.

articles.title: Traders boost US rate-hike bets on hawkish Fed
articles.description: Traders are now fully anticipating a quarter-point interest rate hike from the Federal Reserve by September, a shift driven by renewed inflation worries fueled by a surge in oil prices. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's firm stance against high inflation has bolstered these expectations. This development occurred amidst quiet trading, with US markets closed for a holiday.

articles.title: World Bank clears $1.5 bn package for India's reforms
articles.description: The World Bank has approved a $1.5 billion financing package for India, aimed at accelerating structural reforms and stimulating private sector-led job creation. This initiative supports economic growth by fostering entrepreneurship and simplifying business processes, building on recent advancements in tax and regulatory frameworks. The funding is expected to create millions of job opportunities for young people entering the workforce annually, reflecting India's progress in governance and inclusive labor empowerment.

articles.title: Dollar firms as US-Iran peace deal hangs in balance; yen nears 40-year low
articles.description: The dollar remains strong against most currencies, nearing a 40-year low against the yen, as a potential US-Iran peace deal hangs in the balance. Federal Reserve projections hinting at a year-end rate hike are boosting the dollar, with traders watching closely for any intervention by Japanese authorities to support their currency. Despite a recent Bank of Japan rate hike, Japanese interest rates remain significantly lower than global counterparts.

articles.title: Delhi HC rules EY US secondment fees taxable in India, MNCs face higher costs for foreign talent
articles.description: Multinational corporations in India face a new tax challenge following a High Court ruling against EY US. The court determined that payments for employees deputed to India constitute taxable 'foreign technical services' (FTS), not mere cost reimbursements. This decision could increase the cost of bringing in foreign expertise by up to 10%, prompting companies to re-evaluate their secondment arrangements and potential GST liabilities.

articles.title: Alembic Pharma gets tentative approval from USFDA for generic cancer drug
articles.description: Alembic Pharmaceuticals Ltd on Friday said it has received tentative approval from the US health regulator for its generic version of cancer treatment drug Binimetinib tablets. The tentative approval by the US Food & Drug Administration (USFDA) is for the Abbreviated New Drug Application (ANDA) of Binimetinib tablets of strength 45 mg, Alembic Pharmaceuticals said in a regulatory filing. Based on USFDA's paragraph IV certifications list, Alembic is the sole first applicant to have filed its ANDA for Binimetinib Tablets, 45mg and upon final approval of this ANDA by the USFDA, the company may be eligible for 180 days of generic marketing exclusivity in the US, the company said. Alembic had previously received tentative approval for Binimetinib Tablets, 15mg, it added. Binimetinib in combination with encorafenib is used for the treatment of patients with unresectable or metastatic melanoma with resistance to specific targeted therapies in cancer. It is also indicated, in combination

articles.title: NRI demand for term insurance doubles in two years
articles.description: Term Insurance: NRI demand for term insurance in India has doubled as young overseas Indians seek financial security amid global uncertainty.
